title: Switch labels should not be nested inside non-switch blocks
description: Switch labels should not be nested inside non-switch blocksAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearmaintainabilityCode Smellmisra-c++2008misra-c2004confusingmisra-c2012Why is this an issue?More InfoA switch-label can be placed anywhere within the statements that form the body of a switch statement, potentially leading to unstructured code. To prevent this from happening, the scope of a case-label or default-label shall be the statement forming the body of a switch statement. All case-clauses and the default-clause shall be at the same scope.Noncompliant code exampleswitch (x) { case 1: // Compliant if (foo) { case 2: // Noncompliant break; default: // Noncompliant break; } break; default: // Compliant break; }Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Switch labels should not be nested inside non-switch blocks
